mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in new housing starts indicates supply chain bottlenecks colliding with resilient demand, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample observations, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, a decline in real savings deposits reduces household liquidity and dampens equity risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when real savings fall during high-tightening months, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations and lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 100,000 for two consecutive months, it confirms a labor market recession that triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 100000 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the payroll threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening aggregate supply that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when industrial output falls during high-inflation months, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm employment expands the consumer spending base and supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in employment growth months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
